summary: The pull request fixes nil handling in enterprise member removal by adding input validation and changing the behavior when a member is not found. Previously, when a member was not found, the function returned nil, but now it returns a specific error (ErrMemberNotFound). Additionally, input validation was added at the API layer to check for invalid enterpriseID or userID values.

findings.title: Semantic change in function return behavior
findings.comment: The function previously returned nil when a member was not found, but now returns ErrMemberNotFound. This is a semantic change that could break callers expecting nil return values.

findings.title: API layer input validation added
findings.comment: Input validation was added at the API layer to check for non-positive enterpriseID or userID values, which may affect how invalid requests are handled compared to previous behavior.

findings.title: New validation for enterprise ID
findings.comment: Added validation to check that enterpriseID is not zero before proceeding with deletion logic, which could affect downstream behavior if enterpriseID was previously allowed to be zero.

suggestions: Ensure that all callers of DeleteEnterpriseMember are properly handling the new ErrMemberNotFound error
Verify that the change in behavior from returning nil to returning an error does not break existing clients
Consider adding unit tests specifically for the case where a member is not found to confirm correct error handling
